htmlMain:
A BIG THANK YOU TO OUR GENEROUS SUPPORT FROM OUR MEMBERS AND FRIENDS

SAVE THE DATES FOR OUR ANNUAL SHOP WITH A COP EVENT: Dec 3rd and Dec 9th
Many of our RHBA Members know about this annual event, for those who don’t, to learn more
DONATE (RHBA will match funds up to $750) – VOLUNTEER (2 Opportunities to serve: 12/3 and 12/9) To date we have reached approximately 1/3 of our goal. Your donations, support and volunteerism is an important facet of the services we provide to our local community.
RHBA matches donations made by our members and friends up to $750.00 for to a collective amount of $1500.00 Our RHBA member Amy Swallow is taking the lead on this years SWAC project email amy@stuffadjust.com or call
https://www.stuffadjust.com 503-740-2406
To donate, volunteer or learn more about the SWAC Project visit the link below or email us at: info@raleighhillsbusinessassn.org
Donations/Fund Raising Options
You can also send a check to: RHBA 10445 SW Canyon Rd, Suite 116, Beaverton OR 97005Or bring cash or check to the RHBA Coffee on Nov 12th or to Lunch on Nov 19th.
Jump on Board for the SWAC Guessing Game, created by Amy Swallow!!!
How does a container of candy help SWAC?

Annual Shop With A Cop was another successful event. RHBA partners with Washington County Sheriff’s Foundation every December.

Providing 15 students (K-5) from Raleigh Park Elementary School. Giving the students the opportunity to partner with Washington County Deputies for shopping trip helping make their families’ holiday a little brighter.

Contact RHBA for more info: info@RaleighHillsBusinessAssn.org

This is our biggest social action project of the year. For more than ten years, RHBA has supported a cooperative project with Beaverton School District and Washington County Sheriff’s Office. This project creates a holiday experience for 15 Raleigh Park Elementary School families. RHBA matches donations made by RHBA members and friends up to $750 for a collective amount of $1,500. The donations help purchase gifts for all members of  the 15 families. Other members donate by providing lunch for the kids with the deputies, craft items to make during lunch, gift wrapping, and shopping for the selected students. It is a great big project with many moving parts that offers lots of opportunity to participate.

We have members who were Shop with a Cop recipients when they kids and speak about how much the program meant to them and their families.

So what can you do?

  • Make a donation. You can do it online at the RHBA website. Scroll down and on the right hand side look for a blue lettered Shop with a Cop and that will lead you to the donation page. All donations needed!
  • If shopping is your thing, the pre-shopping event without deputies and kids but with WCSO staff, is happening on a selected date in early December. Meet at Fred Meyer on 11425 SW Beaverton-Hillsdale Hwy. Six volunteers needed.
  • Like to gift wrap…there’s a big wrapping party on following selected date in December 3rd. Seven Volunteers needed.
  • Shopping with kids and deputies at Beaverton Fred Meyer at 11:30am. Followed by lunch at Raleigh Park School at 12:30. Five volunteers needed to set up lunch at school.
  • Reply to info@RaleighHillsBusinessAssn.org ! Times confirmed to all interested parties.
